topic: Activepieces Review 2026: The Open-Source Zapier That's Actually Free

competitors.title: ActivePieces Review (2026): AI-First Open-Source Automation
key_points: Overall rating 4.1/5 - strong for technical founders, AI-forward teams, security-sensitive organizations
AI-first design with native AI models and AI agents inside flows
Visual flow builder with optional code steps - hybrid no-code/low-code approach
Pieces (integrations) are community-extensible open ecosystem
Self-hosting option gives data sovereignty and cost predictability
Human-in-the-loop features for approval steps and to-do pauses
Flexible execution model - no strict per-task metering like Zapier/Make
Cons: Smaller integration library than Zapier/Make, less polished UX, documentation gaps

competitors.title: Activepieces Review 2026: Pros, Cons & Verdict | Automation Atlas
key_points: Score 7.5/10 - strong value at price point
MIT-licensed Community Edition is genuinely free with unlimited tasks
Per-flow task counting: 5-step flow = 1 task (vs 5 on Zapier), making cloud plans 3-5x more generous
Cloud Free: 1,000 tasks, Pro: 10,000 tasks at $5/month
250+ integration pieces as of March 2026
Self-hosting requires only Docker and PostgreSQL, ~15 min deployment on basic VPS
Handles 5,000-10,000 flow executions/day on 2-vCPU VPS
Cons: No native version control, limited error handling, no built-in AI/LLM nodes, no sub-flow architecture
Real deployment: 20-person agency running 52 flows, cost $6/month VPS vs $73.50/month on Zapier

competitors.title: ActivePieces Review 2026: The Best Open-Source Workflow Automation Platform
key_points: Rating 4.8/5 - best for mid-market teams, self-hosted deployments
300+ pre-built integrations, more added monthly
Free tier: $0/month, unlimited tasks, up to 5 active flows, 1 user
Pro: $29/month (billed annually) - unlimited flows, 50K tasks/month, 5 team members
Business: $99/month - 500K tasks, 25 team members, advanced analytics
Enterprise: custom pricing with SSO, on-premise deployment, SLAs
Comparison: ActivePieces self-hosting vs Zapier saves $200-500/month for mid-market teams
Compliance-heavy orgs (healthcare, fintech, government) benefit from self-hosting on VPC
Key differentiator: true data ownership via self-hosting - essential for regulated industries

competitors.title: ActivePieces Review: Open-Source Automation with AI Agents and Unlimited Tasks
key_points: MIT license allows commercial use without restrictions (vs n8n's AGPLv3)
AI Agents concept - workflows can think and act autonomously using LLMs
Model Context Protocol (MCP) support for external AI systems like Claude
Integrated Tables feature for data management within automation platform
330+ integrations as of mid-2025, ~60% community-contributed
SOC 2 compliant for enterprise-grade security
JavaScript/TypeScript code steps and custom npm packages for developers
Docker Compose installation for easy self-hosting
Already executing millions of tasks monthly for thousands of users

stats.fact: Workflow automation market valued at $26.01 billion in 2026, projected to reach $40.77 billion by 2031 at 9.41% CAGR (Mordor Intelligence)
Global workflow automation market projected to grow from $27.91 billion in 2026 to $65.26 billion by 2034 at 11.20% CAGR (Fortune Business Insights)
Activepieces Cloud Free tier: 1,000 tasks/month; Pro: 10,000 tasks at $5/month; self-hosted: unlimited free
Activepieces per-flow task counting makes plans 3-5x more generous than Zapier's per-step counting
Self-hosted Activepieces on 2-vCPU VPS handles 5,000-10,000 flow executions/day
Real deployment: 20-person agency runs 52 flows for $6/month VPS vs $73.50/month on Zapier (85% cost savings)
Activepieces has 250-330+ integration pieces (vs Zapier's 7,000+ and Make's 1,200+)
60% of Activepieces integration pieces are community-contributed
Activepieces Pro plan: $29/month with unlimited flows, 50,000 tasks/month, 5 team members
Self-hosting deployment time approximately 15 minutes on basic VPS (Docker + PostgreSQL)

angles: The 'Actually Free' Angle: Activepieces is the only MIT-licensed automation platform with genuine unlimited tasks on self-hosted - no artificial limits unlike Zapier's 100 tasks/month or n8n's source-available license restrictions
Cost Savings Deep Dive: Real deployment case study shows $6/month VPS running 52 flows vs $73.50/month on Zapier - 85%+ savings for medium workloads
AI-First vs AI-Afterthought: How Activepieces' native AI agent integration and MCP support differ from Zapier/Make bolting on AI as an add-on feature
Per-Flow vs Per-Step Counting: The pricing model innovation that makes Activepieces 3-5x more generous than competitors at equivalent price points
Self-Hosting for Compliance: Activepieces' MIT license makes it uniquely suited for healthcare, fintech, and government orgs that can't use SaaS automation platforms
Growing Pains: Honest assessment of where Activepieces falls short - smaller integration library, documentation gaps, limited error handling, no sub-flow architecture
